body.text: The weekend rewrote what Barnett actually held: the Seventh Circuit dropped its “AR-15s aren’t arms” rationale — and upheld Illinois’ ban anyway on a “particularly dangerous weapons” tradition.

The Reload’s opinion analysis shows the majority assumed AR-15s and 30-round magazines are protected arms, then upheld the ban at Bruen step two — with a 50-page common-use dissent. Also: DOJ’s TRO against California’s “Glock ban” denied with no 2A analysis; GOA sues Pennsylvania.

facts.title: 1. Barnett v. Raoul — 7th Cir. ruling cross-confirmed & reframed
2. Virginia AWB — 8 days to the July 21 statewide injunction

body.text:
• Barnett cross-confirmed: Bevis rationale dropped; ban upheld at step two
• DOJ’s California Glock-ban TRO denied — no 2A analysis; ban in force
• GOA sues PA over lifetime carry ban for 1994 marijuana conviction
• Virginia quiet; injunction official Jul 21; SCOTUS order list Jul 20
